result:
**Goal: Add a "Fluid of Life" mode — a hybrid simulation where Conway's Game of Life cells behave as buoyant particles in a real-time fluid, creating a two-way coupling between cellular automaton logic and Navier-Stokes dynamics.**

Live cells generate heat (buoyancy), dead cells sink. The fluid advects cells to new grid positions before the CA rule is applied, so gliders ride currents, oscillators create vortex streets, and guns pump jets of fluid. Users can place fans, heaters, and walls to sculpt the flow and watch how fluid transport reshapes classic CA behavior — gliders that curve, blinkers that drift, and entirely new emergent structures that exist only at the intersection of discrete life and continuous flow.

This is strategic because the project already has strong CA modes and strong fluid modes independently, but no mode yet fuses them into a single coupled system where each physics layer feeds back into the other.
